FOREST - TROUBLESHOOTING
========================

If Forest.exe won't start at all (you double-click it and "nothing happens"):

  1. Double-click  Forest-Doctor.bat  (right next to Forest.exe).
     It checks every common cause and writes a report to your Desktop
     called  Forest-Doctor-YYYYMMDD-HHMMSS.txt .
     If the report tells you what to fix, do that. If not, send the
     report to the Forest developer.

  2. The two things almost always missing:

       .NET 9 Desktop Runtime (x64)
         https://dotnet.microsoft.com/download/dotnet/9.0
         Pick "Desktop Runtime" -> "x64".
         (Not "ASP.NET Core", not "SDK".)

       WebView2 Runtime
         https://developer.microsoft.com/microsoft-edge/webview2/
         Pick "Evergreen Standalone Installer" (x64).
         (Most Windows 10/11 already have it; install if not.)

  3. Antivirus quarantined Forest.exe / Trees.dll without telling you.
     Check your AV's quarantine list. If Forest is there, restore it
     and add an exclusion for the Forest folder.

  4. EXTRACT the ZIP before running. Running Forest.exe from inside the
     ZIP file (Windows' "preview" mode) often fails silently. Extract
     to a real folder (e.g. C:\Forest) and run it from there.


If Forest opens but a specific feature misbehaves (login, OTP, etc.):

  - Use Forest's "Export Diagnostics" button (Settings) and send the
    resulting zip to the developer. That captures the trees.log with
    the live login state machine.

  - If Forest refuses to launch with a "Trees.dll version mismatch"
    error, your "Trees folder" setting is pointing at an old Forest
    install. Open Settings -> Trees folder and update it to the
    'trees' folder inside this Forest install, or clear the field to
    let Forest auto-detect the bundled trees folder.
